Enable the automatic transfer of requests about a direct report to their new manager
Enable the automatic transfer of requests about a direct report to their new manager

Any feedback requests about a direct report will be automatically forwarded to the new manager in case of a transfer. The new manager will be able to see a read-only version of the replies, as well as the list of users to whom the request was sent. Once the manager transfer is effective, replies to pending requests will be disabled and the requests will no longer be visible to the previous manager.

The review will be published and made visible to the worker after the manager submit their review. beqom recommends that you use the conversation and publication results stage. Please note that this setting cannot be changed after the review form is published.

Only managers are required to provide input. Workers will not be able to view the questions or respond to them.

When you publish the review, it will then be assigned to the workers who are eligible for performance review and who have a line manager within the selected population. To include individual workers, simply search for their name. The system will disregard the workers' eligibility to the review. You can still include more workers to participate after the review is published.

Performance reviews between a worker and their previous manager which have not been started or are in progress will be transferred to the new manager. The previous manager will no longer see the review after its transfer.

Performance reviews between a worker and their previous manager which have not been started or are in progress will be transferred to the new manager. The previous manager will no longer see the review after its transfer.
Allow line managers to reassign a review to another user
Allow line managers to reassign a review to another user

The reassigned reviewer will see the reassigned review in their list and will be able to complete the worker's review. The reassigned reviewer will see all worker-related data (goals, feedback, etc.) visible in the review, the same way as the line manager would. Please note that reassignment implies that the reassigned reviewer will complete the full review process with the worker. If they reassign the review mid-process, their content will not be visible.

The reassigned reviewer will see the reassigned review in their list and will be able to complete the worker's review. The reassigned reviewer will see all worker-related data (goals, feedback, etc.) visible in the review, the same way as the line manager would. A smart search for specific words that are considered unprofessional or deemed sensitive in your organization will be performed. A standard set of words is provided, but the list of words can be customized after activating the settings below, using the “Update sensitive word list” button.

A smart search for specific words that are considered unprofessional or deemed sensitive in your organisation will be performed. A standard set of words is provided, but the list of words can be customised after activating the settings below, using the “Update sensitive word list” button.

The sensitive filter for reviews will be available in the “Manage reviews” section of the performance review template. Sensitive words will be highlighted when viewing the review. In addition, paragraphs containing sensitive content will be specifically accessible by clicking on the sensitive filter icon next to the view review icon.

Here are some tips to make the most of your check-ins.
Here are some tips to make the most of your check-ins.

Make sure you use your check-in time wisely. Coming prepared can greatly benefit the quality of the conversation and also impress your manager, as it demonstrates your focus on your goals and development.

Make sure you use your check-in time wisely. Coming prepared can greatly benefit the quality of the conversation and also impress your manager, as it demonstrates your focus on your goals and development.
The central focus of a check-in should be your current workload, and it should aim at ensuring that you and your manager are aligned on your projects and goals.
The central focus of a check-in should be your current workload, and it should aim at ensuring that you and your manager are aligned on your projects and goals.

To prepare for your check-in, take some notes about the main items you have been working on that week and any key points you want to discuss. It only takes five minutes, but it can significantly improve the quality of your conversation.

Managers will be able to create performance check-ins with their direct reports. The check-ins include widgets in which managers can give their view on a direct report's progress, their overall performance, their goals, and their behaviors. Once the check-in is submitted, workers will be able to see these widgets and decide whether to select "Accept" or "Let's Meet" on the check-in page.

Managers will be able to create performance check-ins with their direct reports. The check-ins include widgets in which managers can give their view on a direct report's progress, their overall performance, their goals, and their behaviours. Once the check-in is submitted, workers will be able to see these widgets and decide whether to select "Accept" or "Let's Meet" on the check-in page.

A Performance Management feature that offers a powerful AI assistant for creating goals and templates from predefined inputs.

A Performance Management feature that enables workers and managers to track professional objectives using specific and measurable goals with achievement metrics and measurable outcomes.

A general application feature that contains a set of widgets offering highlights about key events related to compensation and performance, depending on the features enabled in the platform.

Remember that the focus of your performance review should be the changes you can make to be more impactful in the future. It’s good to reflect on the past, but don't forget to translate it into an action looking forward.

Remember that the focus of your performance review should be the changes you can make to be more impactful in the future. It’s good to reflect on the past, but don't forget to translate it into an action looking forward.
Here are some tips to make the most out of your performance conversation.
Here are some tips to make the most out of your performance conversation.
Performance reviews can be daunting; make sure to address any concerns you have with your manager before the review so that you can have a productive conversation together.
Performance reviews can be daunting; make sure to address any concerns you have with your manager before the review so that you can have a productive conversation together.

Gather your performance data, including feedback from colleagues, progress on your goals, survey reports, and any other relevant data. It is important to analyze your performance data so that you're ready and prepared for your performance review.

Gather your performance data, including feedback from colleagues, progress on your goals, survey reports, and any other relevant data. It is important to analyse your performance data so that you're ready and prepared for your performance review.

Ahead of your performance review, take the time to look back on your progress and your achievements. This is the time to voice how you want to progress in your role and to plan a roadmap with your manager.

Ahead of your performance review, take the time to look back on your progress and your achievements. This is the time to voice how you want to progress in your role and to plan a roadmap with your manager.

Content outside this review period will not appear here, but is accessible in the usual places in the application.

This information is for your reference only and will not be submitted as part of the review. Only the person who performs the reviewer can see the content of this page. This option enables you to suggest an additional manager for this review. After submission, the main manager assigned to this review will decide whether to add your proposed person(s) to the review.

The feedback included here will be visible to {{manager}} when they complete your review. Consider visiting your feedback page to check for individual feedback that could be relevant to your review and that you could share with your manager so that they are visible in this panel.

For your conversation, reflect on your behavior profile, and think about examples of times when you demonstrated each behavior and how you can focus on them in the future. Reflect on any feedback or insights you have received from your colleagues and determine if you can use these to highlight any examples you have. Notice gaps in your wheel? Request feedback on key behaviors now from your peers and stakeholders.

The reporting line is defined by the line manager relationship defined in the data foundation Employment entity.

The reassigned reviewer will see the reassigned process in their list and will be able to complete the manager’s part. A Performance Management feature that enables HRBP administrators to create career development plans in which workers and managers reflect on the events that took place over a defined period of time.

A Performance Management feature that enables workers to schedule regular discussions with their managers and reports to align on goals.

Allow managers to conduct structured discussions with their direct reports. Using dedicated widgets, they can comment on areas such as performance, goals, and behaviors. Once finalized, workers can review comments and either accept the check-in, or propose a follow-up meeting directly from the check-in page.

The configuration for time in role or leave of absence has been modified. Saving these changes will impact future snapshots and related data.

A Leave of absence (LOA) allows workers to take time off work for specific reasons beyond standard paid time off. It may be paid or unpaid, depending on company policies and the purpose of the leave. Common reasons include medical issues, family emergencies, maternity/paternity, education, or personal matters.

A Leave of absence (LOA) allows workers to take time off work for specific reasons beyond standard paid time off. It may be paid or unpaid, depending on company policies and the purpose of the leave. Common reasons include medical issues, family emergencies, maternity/paternity, education, or personal matters.
